claim: Claude Code's creator Boris Cherny stopped writing code by hand in 2026, instead reviewing and merging agent-generated pull requests
actionable: Evaluate your coding workflow to determine if parts can be delegated to agents for generation while you focus on review and verification
source_channel: The AI Frontier | AI for Beginners
source_title: Boris Cherny at Sequoia AI Ascent 2026 — How Claude Code's Creator Stopped Writing Code

claim: Claude Opus 4.7 improved on Cursor Bench from 58% (Opus 4.6) to 70%, representing a significant capability jump in code generation
actionable: When benchmarking coding tools, use Cursor Bench as a standard metric and track improvements across model versions
source_channel: The AI Frontier | AI for Beginners

claim: The scarce skill with AI agents is not manual code writing but knowing what to delegate, what evidence to demand, and when outputs are wrong
actionable: Focus development time on building judgment around task delegation, output verification, and quality assurance rather than manual implementation
source_channel: The AI Frontier | AI for Beginners
source_title: Boris Cherny at Sequoia AI Ascent 2026 — How Claude Code's Creator Stopped Writing Code

claim: Routines (saved prompts with repositories, connectors, and triggers) allow agents to run on schedule or GitHub events from managed cloud infrastructure
actionable: Use Routines to automate recurring coding tasks like PR reviews, documentation updates, and CI feedback loops without manual intervention
source_channel: The AI Frontier | AI for Beginners
source_title: Boris Cherny at Sequoia AI Ascent 2026 — How Claude Code's Creator Stopped Writing Code

claim: Verification of agent outputs scales slower than agent output generation, creating a key bottleneck in autonomous systems
actionable: Design agent systems with human review loops built in; prioritize verification infrastructure as heavily as generation capabilities
source_channel: The AI Frontier | AI for Beginners
